director.photography: Stable 35mm/40mm medium-wide. Advertising-film order. Leave empty frames and windows to suggest someone watching.
soundDesign: Distant lawn mower that stops mid-scene
Single dog bark that cuts off quickly
Phone alert: neighborhood group chat invitation
beats: Iris unloads boxes alone
Evan appears with pie; Mara helps carry a box in
Leasing-office info is suspiciously specific
Mara pushes bulletin board + book club

choices.label: Return the pie plate to Evan
View welcome message in neighborhood group chat

beats: Iris eats the last slice of pie alone
Group-chat invite sits on her phone; curtains are drawn on every house
She decides to explore before settling

N-011.label: Return the pie plate to Evan

director.sceneLocation: INT. IRIS KITCHEN / EXT. EVAN HOUSE
beats: Evan opens the door before Iris rings
He knows she starts work this week (she never told him)
Glimpses inside: red heels, lipstick glass, cold white light under a door
Evan primes 'noises are normal in houses like these'
soundDesign: Distant click before sprinkler system turns on
Very faint low-frequency hum from behind the closed door

beats: Board is overly orderly; cold white light, no one around
'After 10 PM, report through Resident Support. Do not knock on neighbors' doors.'
Courtesy Walk schedule densely lists Evan's name
Formally printed 'Welcome, Iris Lin — Unit 17' already on the board
Rapid welcome messages, weirdly specific ('Loved your yellow lamp')
A 6:08 PM photo pops up: Iris accepting pie, shot from across the street

N-020.label: Abnormal Noise on the First Night

director.sceneLocation: INT. IRIS BEDROOM / WINDOW ON MARA'S SIDE YARD
director.photography: Tilt-to-look: viewer physically lifts a blind-slat to peer out at Mara's side yard. POV embodied.
beats: Metal crash outside; heavy scrape across concrete
Iris lifts a blind slat — reveal: Evan dragging a black body bag into Mara's garage
Group chat: 'Mara is feeling unwell and won't make the book club tomorrow.'
soundDesign: Wall contraction, AC hum, audible breathing
Metal crash → dead silence → dragging sound
Crickets dominate once Iris reaches the window

choices.label: Record the time and keep watching
Leave a voice message for Resident Support

beats: Choice presented over Iris's shaking POV at the window
Screenplay notes: 1, 2, 3 lead to death; 4 leads to survival

D-031.label: Ask in the group "Is Mara okay?"

beats: Instant typing indicators from neighbors, too fast
A just-taken photo of Iris's window appears in the group: 'You're still up.'
Porch lights come on one by one; three soft knocks; bedroom door lock clicks open
Automated line: 'This call may be monitored or shared for community safety.'
Background noise outside cuts out — someone stopped moving
Motion lights snap on, silhouetting Iris in the window
Back-door handle is pressed while male voice calls her to the front door
Evan pauses mid-drag, looks up directly at Iris's window
He smiles and waves across the yard
Doorbell — polite, not urgent — rings twice

director.sceneLocation: EXT. EVAN'S HOUSE SIDE WALL / BASEMENT WINDOW / TRASH CAN
beats: Dark red smear leads from basement window into the house
Wet light-colored fabric caught under a doormat; wine glass with lipstick
Trash: bleach bottle, disposable gloves, blood streak on inner rim
Surveillance camera under eaves is aimed at Iris's own front door
Iris steps on gravel — water inside the house stops
director.photography: Future pass: likely another tilt-look or tap-hotspot scene (examine trash / peek through basement window). Ending placeholder for demo milestone.
